more.title: South African Scientists Shine at 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting in Germany
more.description: South Africa's next generation of scientists is making its mark on the global stage at the 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting in Lindau, Germany, where some of the world's brightest early-career researchers are engaging directly with Nobel Prize winners and leading scientific thinkers. more.title: EXCLUSIVE | Letsoaka urges Safa to retain Broos in advisory role for smooth Bafana Bafana transition
more.description: Former Bafana assistant Serame Letsoaka advises SAFA to retain Hugo Broos temporarily, ensuring a smooth transition and continuity for the national team’s upcoming qualifiers.

more.title: UK Promoter Y2K Entertainment Cuts All Ties With South African Music Industry Over Migrant Crisis Backlash
more.description: South Africa’s music industry has taken a serious international hit. UK-based Y2K Entertainment Promotions, a long-running promoter of Southern African music, announced on 2 July that it is withdrawing all support for South African artists. The Y2K Entertainment boycott is being framed by the company as a direct response to the anti-illegal immigration protests that [...] The post UK Promoter Y2K Entertainment Cuts All Ties With South African Music Industry Over Migrant Crisis Backlash appeared first on SurgeZirc SA .

more.title: Watch: South Africans in stitches as locals learn to ride Sixty60
more.description: The videos are out as the locals learn to ride Sixty60. The reactions are louder. And South Africa is equal parts encouraging and crying laughing, reports Cape {town} Etc. more.title: Springboks face ticket sales challenges ahead of England Test match
more.description: SA Rugby attributes slow ticket sales for the Springboks' opening Test against England to external factors, including the war in Iran, while captain Siya Kolisi emphasises the team's connection with fans.

more.title: Cash at home: why many South Africans still avoid formal savings
more.description: Nearly half of South Africans continue to save cash at home despite wider access to banking services, exposing themselves to theft, fire and lost interest, according to FinMark Trust and World Bank data. Experts say trust, accessibility and financial habits remain major barriers to formal saving.

more.title: Public Ambulance Shortage in South Africa Sparks Parliamentary Outcry
more.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa — A growing public ambulance shortage in South Africa has become a focal point of concern in Parliament, with opposition parties warning that vulnerable citizens are being left behind. more.description: Ethekwini Municipality is gearing up for the 2026 Hollywoodbets Durban July, with Mayor Cyril Xaba highlighting the event's economic impact and support for local talent.

more.title: Boks could lose No 1 spot to the All Blacks. Here's how...
more.description: The Springboks could surrender their world No. 1 ranking on Saturday. Here's every World Rugby rankings permutation ahead of the England Test.

more.title: Liberty study reveals financial strain on mid-career South Africans
more.description: A new Liberty and UCT study reveals that South Africans aged 35 to 55 are increasingly anxious about retirement, job security and rising living costs despite being in their highest earning years. The research shows financial resilience has overtaken wealth as the primary measure of success.

more.title: Jacob Zuma India Visit Sparks Outrage: Government Slams High Commissioner and Fugitive Meetings
more.description: PRETORIA, Gauteng — The highly scrutinized Jacob Zuma India visit has ignited a fierce diplomatic and political firestorm, prompting the South African government to launch an urgent probe into the conduct of its top envoy in New Delhi. Following viral images showing the former president alongside a wanted fugitive, cabinet officials are now threatening drastic consequences [...]
